Auto-attribution · prediction-market signal

Why is moving today?

Type a ticker and press Enter — we'll run /reverse-finder across 1300+ live prediction markets and show the top contributors with their β, t-stat and R².

Live Polymarket data

Regress stock returns on what the market actually believes.

Pick a ticker, choose Polymarket events that should matter, and run an OLS regression on the daily change in their logit-transformed probabilities. Inference uses HAC (heteroskedasticity- and autocorrelation-consistent) standard errors.

Preset workflows · click to run Pre-fills ticker, factors and date range, then fits.

Reverse Factor Finder — which prediction markets best explain your stock?

Modely = α + Σβᵢ Δlogit(pᵢ)

0
0 = contemporaneous (descriptive). ≥1 = predictive: r_t ~ Δlogit_{t−k}.
0.50
0.50 = median. Lower = downside risk; higher = upside.
0 = off. ≥1 = reduce factors to top-k principal components before regressing.
off
Reserve last X% as test. R² on test is the honest predictive metric.
off
Resamples residuals to build non-parametric 95% CIs for each β.
off
Re-fit on a moving window (≥20 days). Plots β(t) — captures regime changes.
off
Tests x → y at each lag: does past Δlogit help predict r?
off
Shuffle each factor's values N times and re-fit on a 80/20 split. The fraction of nulls beating the real test R² is the permutation p-value — a sharper "is this signal or just luck?" check than in-sample R².
Strips market exposure first: r̃ = r − β̂·SPY, then regresses against Polymarket factors. Verified to roughly double test R² for tickers with clear market beta (COIN: +0.16 → +0.36 in our 210-experiment audit).
Averages factors within each theme into ~7 composites instead of regressing on 30+ raw factors. Reduces overfitting dramatically. Permutation test moved 5/10 tickers from "noise" (p≥.20) to "marginal signal" (p<.10).
off
Forces stepwise diversification. 1 = one factor per theme — prevents picking 5 correlated AI factors.

No factors selected. Pick from Curated or Discover.

Selected factor probabilities

Tip: factors with similar history lengths give the largest joint sample. If one curve starts much later than the others, switching to it shrinks the regression window. Aim for matching coverage in your chosen window.

Pinned fits · 0/5

Compare pinned fits

Per-factor β · t · p across pinned fits and current fit. Cells with p < 0.05 are highlighted green.

p < 0.05 Current fit column Esc to close

Save fit to library

Result

Factor coefficients

β with 95% confidence intervals. Click any bar to drill into that factor's series and scatter.

click a bar →
Factor β ? SE ? t ? p ? 95% CI ? VIF ? Clip% ?

Observed vs predicted return

Bars show the spread (observed − predicted). Purple solid bars = under-prediction; orange hatched (diagonal) bars = over-prediction. The fill pattern is a redundant cue for colorblind viewers.

Residuals

Observed minus predicted, by date.

Coefficient stability over time

If β stays inside the ±2σ band, factor exposure is stable. Wide swings flag regime change.

Predicted return decomposition

Per-factor contribution βi · E[Δlogiti] stacked into total predicted return.

Residual normality (Q-Q plot)

Points on the diagonal mean residuals are normal so HAC SEs are valid. Heavy tails suggest robust SE.

Permutation test: is the alpha real?

If observed (red line) sits in the right tail of the null (blue), the model beats randomization.

Bootstrap 95% CI for coefficients

If the lower bound is on the same side of zero as the point estimate, the loading is robustly non-zero.

Factor PCA decomposition

Cumulative variance captured by each principal component of the factor matrix.

Factor correlation matrix

How tightly the factor probabilities co-move. Off-diagonal cells near ±1 inflate VIF and make individual β harder to identify.

Lead-lag relationships (Granger p-values)

Off-diagonal cells: does the row factor lead the return at that lag? p<0.05 means a causal direction is detected.

Factor coefficients with HAC standard errors

Bar length is β. Whiskers are ±1.96·HAC_SE (95% CI). Bars not crossing zero are significant.

Pseudo equity curve in-sample, naive

Daily-rebalanced replay of sign(predicted) over the fit window, charging a flat 5 bp per trade. Ignores factor latency, slippage, and capacity — for shape-of-signal only.

Out-of-sample R² and walk-forward folds in the OOS tab are the honest yardstick — this curve is illustrative only.

Which factors are doing the work?

Leave-one-out R² impact per factor: ΔR²i = R²full − R²without i. Orange bars (Δ ≥ 0.05) are real contributors; grey bars (Δ < 0.01) are redundant and candidates to drop.

Factor probability paths

Underlying pt for each selected market.

Out-of-sample fit

Train on the first 80%, test on the last 20%. Shaded region is the held-out test sample.

Rolling β over time

Each line shows βi(t) computed on a moving window. Sustained shifts indicate regime changes.

Granger causality · lags

F-test p-value per (factor, lag). p < 0.05 means past Δlogit at that lag does help predict the return.

Bootstrap 95% CI

Non-parametric residual bootstrap. CIs that exclude 0 indicate robustly non-zero β.

Principal components

Variance explained per PC + factor loadings on each PC.

Per-factor Δlogit stationarity

Each Δlogit series should be stationary on its own; a factor with drift can drag the regression.

Spread stationarity

ADF (null = unit root) and KPSS (null = stationary) on the residuals. When both agree, the verdict is robust. A non-stationary residual flags a possibly spurious regression.

Variance Inflation Factor

VIF > 5 indicates problematic multicollinearity.

Permutation null distribution

Histogram of test R² under N independent factor-shuffles. The vertical line marks the real test R². If it sits in the right tail, the model has picked up something that survives random reassignment of factor values.

Event vs Events

Regress one prediction-market event against other events to quantify how they co-move. Coefficients are betas of Δlogit on Δlogit; HAC standard errors.

Advanced models

Non-linear, conditional, regime-switching, and tail-dependency models for event-stock relationships.

Multi-event

Decompose ticker returns or sector returns across many prediction-market factors simultaneously, or find chains.

Alpha Strategies

Curated quantitative strategies derived from prediction-market signals. Each pair has been screened through Engle-Granger cointegration, walk-forward out-of-sample Sharpe, Benjamini-Hochberg multiple-testing correction and a four-quarter stability gate. Click any row to read the full report.

Settings

Email Alerts off
Send notifications when the engine detects a profitable arb above the threshold.
Scan Mode OG
REST polling (OG, 8s) or WebSocket push (WS, real-time).
Threshold 0.94
Maximum combined cost (K + P) to flag as arb. 0.94 = require 6% gross edge.
Min Alert Profit 1.0%
Mute alerts below this net profit (after fees).

Crypto model vs market

Our probability for every Polymarket BTC / ETH 5m and 15m up-down boundary, shown next to the market's price. Model = GBM with σ blended from a 30-day Binance daily-close estimate and (when available) cryptostuff's live tick σ, plus a drift bias from order-flow imbalance capped at ±30%/yr.

—

Model vs market · BTC/ETH · 5m

—
How this works
We compute a GBM probability up-down for each window. σ blends a 30-day Binance daily-close estimate with cryptostuff's live tick-σ (variance-weighted), and adds an OFI-derived drift bias capped at ±30%/yr plus a smaller whale-flow term. When |z_vwap|>2 the drift is shrunk toward 0 and a mean-reversion pull replaces it. Signal fires when the model-vs-market gap |Δ| ≥ 5%; size with Kelly bounded at 0.25.

Our probability vs the market's for every Polymarket 5m / 15m up-down boundary. Signal fires when |Δ| ≥ 5%. When Polymarket has no open market for a combo, our model probability still shows.

Each card resolves at the next exact 5m / 15m UTC boundary. Auto-refreshes every 4s while visible. live σ from cryptostuff fallback σ from Binance daily

10-pair Binance snapshot

Live Edge

Real-time z-score signals across the curated alpha pairs, sourced from web/data/live_signals.json. LONG SPREAD / SHORT SPREAD are actionable entries when |z| crosses the entry threshold; HOLD is shown for in-position pairs; FLAT EXIT when |z| drops below the exit band. Click any row to open the A-leg market in Terminal mode.

Loading live signals…

Auto-Backtest scan + backtest pipeline

One-click pipeline: scan the catalog for cointegrated pairs, backtest each with z-score signals, rank by Sharpe. The full research workflow in a single click. Click any leaderboard row to drill down into Cointegration / Pairs Trading / OU Bands tabs pre-loaded.

Quick start — by theme

Inefficiency Scanner cartesian · 3 tracks

Walks every pair in the catalog (or a theme subset) and surfaces three independent leaderboards: logical-implication violations, conditional dependencies (HAC-OLS β with surprise penalty), and cointegrated pairs (Engle-Granger ADF + half-life).

Try a theme

Pair Explorer P(A) ↔ P(B) diagnostics

Distribution-free tests on a single factor pair: logical-implication violation, HAC-OLS conditional regression, Fréchet–Hoeffding bounds. Click any leaderboard row in the Scanner to pre-fill these inputs.

Quick start — backtested presets

Cointegration Engle-Granger 2-step

Test whether the spread P(A) − β·P(B) is stationary. Reports the OLS hedge ratio β, the ADF p-value on the residuals, and the AR(1)-derived half-life of mean reversion. A short half-life with a rejected ADF is the green light for the pairs-trading backtest.

Quick start — pairs the scanner already verified

Kalman Hedge time-varying β_t

Kalman filter on state-space model β_t = β_{t-1} + η_t, y_t = β_t·x_t + ε_t. The hedge ratio adapts to regime changes — better than static OLS β when the cointegrating relationship drifts. Smaller δ ⇒ slower; larger δ ⇒ more responsive. The filter's *innovation* e_t is the adaptive spread.

Quick start — Kalman scenarios

Pairs Trading Backtest z-score signals · walk-forward

Walk-forward backtest of a z-score-based mean-reversion trade on the Engle-Granger spread. Long-spread when z < −entry, short when z > +entry, exit at |z| < exit, stop at |z| ≥ stop. Reports Sharpe, hit-rate, max drawdown, holding period.

Quick start — multi-factor scenarios

Trade ledger

Event Model HAC-OLS · explanatory

Regress one event's probability on N other event probabilities with HAC (heteroskedasticity- and autocorrelation-consistent) standard errors. Tells you which markets *jointly explain* a target market — and what's left over (the residual is the idiosyncratic / news component, the "alpha" leg of the basket).

Quick start — multi-factor explanatory models

Basket Stat-Arb PCA residuals · Avellaneda-Lee

Stack a basket of related event probabilities, subtract the top-k principal components, and trade the rolling z-score of the *residuals*. Generalises pairs trading to k > 2. Reports per-market Kelly fractions and a residual heatmap.

Quick start — pre-built strike-family baskets

OU Bands OU-optimal thresholds

Calibrate continuous-time Ornstein-Uhlenbeck dynamics dX = κ(μ − X)dt + σdW on the cointegration spread, then derive the analytically-optimal entry / exit z-thresholds that maximise expected PnL per unit time net of round-trip transaction cost from a continuous-time OU calibration. Strictly-better than picking 2σ/0.5σ by hand.

Quick start — OU-tuned cointegrating pairs

Mean-Reversion Diagnostics Hurst R/S · variance ratio

Two model-free tests on a single factor's probability path: Hurst exponent via R/S analysis (H<0.5 mean-reverting, H≈0.5 random walk, H>0.5 trending) and the variance-ratio test with heteroscedasticity-consistent z-stat. Use to verify a candidate spread is actually reverting before sizing a pairs trade.

Causality (Lead / Follower) leader / follower

Bidirectional causality test (lead vs follower) in *both* directions. Identifies which event's news leads the other. Use it to size event-driven trades: long the follower, hedge with the leader.

Quick start — leadership detection examples

Spot vs market-implied Binance · GBM · YZ vol

Compares a live underlying (Binance daily or 5-min klines, no auth) against a Polymarket-style binary outcome. Vol via Yang-Zhang OHLC; closed-form GBM for terminal / one-touch geometries; bootstrap CI on the model probability. Caveat: Polymarket BTC markets typically settle on Coinbase / UMA — Binance ≠ Coinbase, expect 1-5 bps basis near the strike.

Quick start — live-data scenarios

Alpha-deployability reports versioned

Versioned narratives of every wave's backtest / robustness gate. Each docs/alpha-reports/alpha-report-vN.md documents which strategies graduated to A_GOLD / B_VALIDATED vs which were demoted. v22 is the current Wave-7 4Q-stability reckoning (2026-05-19) — found 0 of 5 Wave-6 A_STRUCTURAL promotions clear the strict 4-quarter Sharpe-stability gate (none had > 174 joint days of Polymarket history). Older versions kept for audit. Click a report to open the raw markdown.

Loading report summary…

Factor Galaxy classical MDS · descriptive

A 2-D map of the prediction-market universe. Each point is a factor; distance is 1 − |corr| of daily Δlogit moves embedded by classical (Torgerson) MDS, so distance in the plot is honest — close points genuinely co-move. Colours are agglomerative co-movement clusters; point size is Δlogit volatility. This is unsupervised structure, not a forecast: use it to de-dup baskets, find hedges, and spot lone-wolf factors.

Mispricing Scanner relative value · z-score

For each factor we regress its Δlogit on its most-correlated neighbors and standardize the latest residual. A large |z| means the contract is trading rich or cheap versus what its peers imply — a relative-value flag, not a directional forecast. Ranked by |z|.

Factor Importance gradient-boosted · diagnostic

Fits a gradient-boosted model of a stock's daily log returns on prediction-market factors and ranks them by permutation importance under walk-forward CV. This is a diagnostic that complements the linear factor model in Regression mode — not a tradeable forecast. Watch the out-of-sample R²: a negative value means there's no exploitable signal, which is an honest and common result.

Regime Monitor Gaussian mixture · state

Classifies each day's market state from cross-sectional dispersion, activity, co-movement and volatility, then flags the current regime. Every anti-alpha this project buried died from a regime flip — this panel tells you whether a strategy is operating in the regime it was validated in. Regimes are vol-ordered: calm → normal → stressed.

Event Graph VAR · Granger lead-lag

A network of events. Soft grey links = contemporaneous co-movement ("move at the same time"); coloured arrows = directed lead-lag from a VAR Granger test ("this event drives that one", with the lag in days). Node size = how many others it drives (influence); colour = community; layout matches the Factor Galaxy. Each node also carries a VAR model-implied fair price vs market — trust the mispricing only when the out-of-sample R² is positive.

Calibration & Favorite–Longshot resolved markets · ground truth

Prediction markets resolve, so we get free labels: is a 70¢ contract right 70% of the time? The reliability curve below plots empirical YES-rate vs price — points below the diagonal mean the market overpays. The classic edge is the favorite–longshot bias: longshots tend to be overpriced. The isotonic calibrated curve is the model's fair-probability map.

Latent Factor Model PCA · risk decomposition

Decomposes the whole prediction-market universe into a few latent risk factors (e.g. an "election bloc", a "rate-cut bloc"). The scree bars show how much each explains; each principal component is labelled by its strongest +/− members. What's left over per contract is its idiosyncratic residual — a cleaner relative-value signal than pairwise, ranked by |z| below.

Triple-Barrier Meta-Label take / skip filter

Doesn't invent a new alpha — it filters an existing one. A primary mean-reversion signal fires on z-score; each trigger is labelled win/lose with a triple-barrier (profit / stop / time), and a walk-forward classifier learns which triggers to take. The payoff is a higher hit-rate on fewer, better trades. Watch the out-of-fold AUC — > 0.5 means the filter has real skill.

Live · Polymarket + Kalshi · 1,250 contracts

Browse every market in a Bloomberg-style terminal

Theme heatmap, live movers, orderbook depth and cointegrated peers — drill into any contract in two clicks.

news
6 endpoints · data hub
Peer Scanner
Comparable contracts by theme / co-movement. Returns ranked peers with tier badges and a best-pair pick.
Quality Score
Composite liquidity / depth / spread / age score (0-100) with letter grade and component breakdown.
Vol Surface
Implied-vol cone from prediction-market odds (logit-σ, 1d/7d/30d/90d). Needs cached price history.
Trades Tape
Last 50 prints (price / size / side) tagged AT_MID / BUY / SELL from the Polymarket CLOB.
Screener
Filter the factor catalog by free-text query and theme. Returns ranked matches with slug + theme + score.
Portfolio Simulator
Multi-leg book PnL over a daily history window. One position per line as slug,SIDE,size_usd (e.g. us-recession-by-end-of-2026,YES,1000). Returns Sharpe, max-drawdown, and pairwise correlation.
Implied PDF · S&P 500 / Nasdaq-100
Risk-neutral terminal distribution recovered from the Kalshi index range-market ladder (arbitrage-free PCHIP CDF, differentiated to a density). Per-contract overround is removed by renormalisation. SPX ≈ what SPY tracks.
Pricing Kernel · Kalshi vs Options
Overlays three terminal distributions of the index: Kalshi-Q (prediction-market risk-neutral), Options-Q (risk-neutral density from the ^SPX chain — the second derivative of the call-price curve, with monotone+convex shape restrictions), and Physical-P (GARCH). Shows the cross-venue divergence and the empirical pricing kernel M(S)=e-rτ·fQ/fP with implied risk aversion.
